result.stdout:
CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  A CLI for interacting with the Beamable Cloud.

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools [command] [options]

Options:
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  --version                                  Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  me                                        Fetch the current account
  checks                                    Check if your projects for known issues
  config                                    List the current beamable configuration
  content                                   Open content folder in file explorer
  deploy, deployment, deployments, deploys  Commands for interacting with microservice and 
                                            microstorage deployments
  developer-user-manager                    The command that manages the developer users
  fed, federation                           Commands to work with microservice federation
  init <path>                               Initialize a new Beamable project in the current 
                                            directory [default: .]
  listen                                    Listen to events
  login                                     Save credentials
  logout                                    Removes any saved credentials
  oapi                                      Commands that integrate the Beamable API and open API
  org                                       Commands related to beamable organizations
  player                                    Get information about a player
  portal                                    Open portal
  project                                   Commands that relate to a standalone Beamable project
  otel, tel, telemetry                      Allows access to Open Telemetry related commands
  temp                                      Commands for the .beamable temp folder
  version                                   Commands for managing the CLI version

                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally

                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp

                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment

  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will

                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE

  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to

  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a

                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  archive-manifest <manifest-id>  Archive an existing content manifest
  list-manifests                  List all local and remote content manifest ids
  new-manifest <manifest-id>      Create a local empty content manifest for the current realm
  open                            Open content file in default editor
  ps                              List the running status of local services not running in docker
  publish                         Publish content and manifest
  replace-local                   Replaces the local content from a specific realm cached folder to 
                                  another realm cached folder | You will lose all the content in 
                                  the target realm
  resolve                         Resolve between local changes ore realm based changes
  restore                         Restore content data based on a local snapshot, remote manifest 
                                  ID or manifest file
  save                            Saves a serialized content properties JSON-blob into a manifest 
                                  (expects the blob to be in Beamable's Serialization Format). This 
                                  command is not meant for manual usage. It is meant for engine 
                                  integrations and CI/CD content enforcing use-cases.Editing of 
                                  content is to be made either via engine integrations OR via a 
                                  JSON text-editor
  snapshot                        Save a manifest as a contents snapshot
  snapshot-list                   Find and list all shared (.beamable/content-snapshots/[PID]) and 
                                  local (.beamable/temp/content-snapshots/[PID]) snapshots
  status                          Show current status of the content
  sync                            Synchronizes the local content matching the filters to the latest 
                                  content stored in the realm
  tag                             Commands for adding/set/removing tags

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Open content file in default editor

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools content open [options]

Options:
  --manifest-ids <manifest-ids>                     Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids 
                                                    for which to return data. By default, will 
                                                    return just the global manifest [default: 
                                                    global]
  --filter-type                                     Defines the semantics for the `filter` 
  <ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects 
  hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
                                                    ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters
                                                    Regexes => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content whose Id is matched by any of the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
                                                    TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content of the ','-separated list of filters 
                                                    (content type strings with full hierarchy --- 
                                                    StartsWith comparison)
                                                    Tags => Will add the given tags to any content 
                                                    that currently has any of the ','-separated 
                                                    list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]
  --filter <filter>                                 Accepts different strings to filter which 
                                                    content files will be affected. See the 
                                                    `filter-type` option []
  --dryrun                                          [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as 
                                                    possible without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                       CID (CustomerId) to use (found in 
                                                    Portal->Account); defaults to whatever is in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                                 If passed, sets the engine integration that is 
                                                    calling for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>         The version of the Beamable's SDK running in 
                                                    that Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>                 The version of the engine that is calling the 
                                                    CLI
  --pid <pid>                                       PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games 
                                                    -> Any Realm's details); defaults to whatever 
                                                    is in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                       When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                                    arguments (or error if no defaults are 
                                                    possible) [default: False]
  --host <host>                                     This option defines the target Beamable 
                                                    environment. Needed for private cloud customers 
                                                    to target their exclusive Beamable environment. 
                                                    Ignorable by everyone else. Stored in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>                     The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>                   A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                               Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                                     If there is a local dotnet tool installation 
                                                    (with a ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for 
                                                    the beam tool, then any global invocation of 
                                                    the beam tool will automatically redirect and 
                                                    call the local version. However, there will be 
                                                    a performance penalty due to the extra process 
                                                    invocation. This option flag will cause an 
                                                    error to occur instead of automatically 
                                                    redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                                    invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation                  By default, any local CLI invocation that 
                                                    should trigger a Federation of any type will 
                                                    prefer locally running Microservices. However, 
                                                    if you need the CLI to use the remotely running 
                                                    Microservices, use this option to ignore 
                                                    locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                     By default, logs will automatically mask 
                                                    tokens. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    tokens will be visible in their full text. This 
                                                    is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                     By default, logs are automatically written to a 
                                                    temp file so that they can be used in an error 
                                                    case. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    logs are not written. Also, if the 
                                                    BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment variable is 
                                                    set, no log file will be written.  [default: 
                                                    False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>               a custom location for docker. By default, the 
                                                    CLI will attempt to resolve docker through its 
                                                    usual install locations. You can also use the 
                                                    BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to 
                                                    specify. 
                                                    Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                                    identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                                Out all log messages as data payloads in 
                                                    addition to however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>             additional file paths to be included when 
                                                    building a local project manifest.
  --raw                                             Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens 
                                                    by default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                          Output syntax highlighted box text. This 
                                                    happens by default when the command is not 
                                                    piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                       a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Resolve between local changes ore realm based changes

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools content resolve [options]

Options:
  --manifest-ids <manifest-ids>                     Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids 
                                                    for which to return data. By default, will 
                                                    return just the global manifest [default: 
                                                    global]
  --filter-type                                     Defines the semantics for the `filter` 
  <ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects 
  hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
                                                    ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters
                                                    Regexes => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content whose Id is matched by any of the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
                                                    TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content of the ','-separated list of filters 
                                                    (content type strings with full hierarchy --- 
                                                    StartsWith comparison)
                                                    Tags => Will add the given tags to any content 
                                                    that currently has any of the ','-separated 
                                                    list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]
  --filter <filter>                                 Accepts different strings to filter which 
                                                    content files will be affected. See the 
                                                    `filter-type` option []
  --use <use>                                       Whether to use the 'local' or 'realm' version 
                                                    of the conflicted content.
                                                    This applies to ALL matching elements of the 
                                                    filter that are conflicted.
                                                    Value must be "local" or "realm"
  --dryrun                                          [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as 
                                                    possible without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                       CID (CustomerId) to use (found in 
                                                    Portal->Account); defaults to whatever is in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                                 If passed, sets the engine integration that is 
                                                    calling for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>         The version of the Beamable's SDK running in 
                                                    that Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>                 The version of the engine that is calling the 
                                                    CLI
  --pid <pid>                                       PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games 
                                                    -> Any Realm's details); defaults to whatever 
                                                    is in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                       When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                                    arguments (or error if no defaults are 
                                                    possible) [default: False]
  --host <host>                                     This option defines the target Beamable 
                                                    environment. Needed for private cloud customers 
                                                    to target their exclusive Beamable environment. 
                                                    Ignorable by everyone else. Stored in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>                     The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>                   A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                               Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                                     If there is a local dotnet tool installation 
                                                    (with a ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for 
                                                    the beam tool, then any global invocation of 
                                                    the beam tool will automatically redirect and 
                                                    call the local version. However, there will be 
                                                    a performance penalty due to the extra process 
                                                    invocation. This option flag will cause an 
                                                    error to occur instead of automatically 
                                                    redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                                    invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation                  By default, any local CLI invocation that 
                                                    should trigger a Federation of any type will 
                                                    prefer locally running Microservices. However, 
                                                    if you need the CLI to use the remotely running 
                                                    Microservices, use this option to ignore 
                                                    locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                     By default, logs will automatically mask 
                                                    tokens. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    tokens will be visible in their full text. This 
                                                    is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                     By default, logs are automatically written to a 
                                                    temp file so that they can be used in an error 
                                                    case. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    logs are not written. Also, if the 
                                                    BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment variable is 
                                                    set, no log file will be written.  [default: 
                                                    False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>               a custom location for docker. By default, the 
                                                    CLI will attempt to resolve docker through its 
                                                    usual install locations. You can also use the 
                                                    BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to 
                                                    specify. 
                                                    Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                                    identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                                Out all log messages as data payloads in 
                                                    addition to however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>             additional file paths to be included when 
                                                    building a local project manifest.
  --raw                                             Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens 
                                                    by default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                          Output syntax highlighted box text. This 
                                                    happens by default when the command is not 
                                                    piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                       a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

Raw Output:
  Returns a stream of ContentResolveConflictResult object on the stream stream, which each may resemble the following...
  {
    "ts": 1774693739131,
    "type": "stream",
    "data": {
      "ResolvedContentIds": null
    }
  }

Description:

                                             will be restored. The default value is `global` 
                                             [default: global]
  -n, --name <name>                          Defines the name or path for the snapshot to be 
                                             restored. If passed a name, it will first get the 
                                             snapshot from shared folder 
                                             '.beamable/content-snapshots/[PID]' than from the 
                                             local only under 
                                             '.beamable/temp/content-snapshots/[PID]'. If a path is 
                                             passed, it is going to try get the json file from the 
                                             path []
  --pid <pid>                                An optional field to set the PID from where you would 
                                             like to get the snapshot to be restored. The default 
                                             will be the current PID the user are in []
  -d, --delete-after-restore                 Defines if the snapshot file should be deleted after 
                                             restoring [default: False]
  --additive-restore                         Defines if the restore will additionally adds the 
                                             contents without deleting current local contents 
                                             [default: False]
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account);

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version.

                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will

                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE

                                             however they are logged

  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by

result.stdout:
CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Saves a serialized content properties JSON-blob into a manifest (expects the blob to be in 
  Beamable's Serialization Format). This command is not meant for manual usage. It is meant for 
  engine integrations and CI/CD content enforcing use-cases.Editing of content is to be made either 
  via engine integrations OR via a JSON text-editor

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools content save [options]

Options:
  --manifest-ids <manifest-ids>              Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids for 
                                             which to return data. By default, will return just the 
                                             global manifest [default: global]
  --content-ids <content-ids>                An array of existing content ids []
  --content-properties <content-properties>  An array, parallel to the --content-ids, that contain 
                                             the escaped properties json for each content
  --force                                    When this is set, this will ignore your local state 
                                             and save the properties directly to disk
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out

                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.

                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]

                                             environment variable to specify.

                                             identified. [default: docker]

                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Synchronizes the local content matching the filters to the latest content stored in the realm

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools content sync [options]

Options:
  --manifest-ids <manifest-ids>                     Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids 
                                                    for which to return data. By default, will 
                                                    return just the global manifest [default: 
                                                    global]
  --filter-type                                     Defines the semantics for the `filter` 
  <ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects 
  hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
                                                    ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters
                                                    Regexes => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content whose Id is matched by any of the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
                                                    TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content of the ','-separated list of filters 
                                                    (content type strings with full hierarchy --- 
                                                    StartsWith comparison)
                                                    Tags => Will add the given tags to any content 
                                                    that currently has any of the ','-separated 
                                                    list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]
  --filter <filter>                                 Accepts different strings to filter which 
                                                    content files will be affected. See the 
                                                    `filter-type` option []
  --sync-created                                    Deletes any created content that is not present 
                                                    in the latest manifest. If filters are 
                                                    provided, will only delete the created content 
                                                    that matches the filter
  --sync-modified                                   This will discard your local changes ONLY on 
                                                    files that are NOT conflicted. If filters are 
                                                    provided, will only do this for content that 
                                                    matches the filter
  --sync-conflicts                                  This will discard your local changes ONLY on 
                                                    files that ARE conflicted. If filters are 
                                                    provided, will only do this for content that 
                                                    matches the filter
  --sync-deleted                                    This will revert all your deleted files. If 
                                                    filters are provided, will only do this for 
                                                    content that matches the filter
  --target <target>                                 If you pass in a Manifest's UID, we'll sync 
                                                    with that as the target. If filters are 
                                                    provided, will only do this for content that 
                                                    matches the filter
  --dryrun                                          [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as 
                                                    possible without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                       CID (CustomerId) to use (found in 
                                                    Portal->Account); defaults to whatever is in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                                 If passed, sets the engine integration that is 
                                                    calling for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>         The version of the Beamable's SDK running in 
                                                    that Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>                 The version of the engine that is calling the 
                                                    CLI
  --pid <pid>                                       PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games 
                                                    -> Any Realm's details); defaults to whatever 
                                                    is in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                       When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                                    arguments (or error if no defaults are 
                                                    possible) [default: False]
  --host <host>                                     This option defines the target Beamable 
                                                    environment. Needed for private cloud customers 
                                                    to target their exclusive Beamable environment. 
                                                    Ignorable by everyone else. Stored in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>                     The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>                   A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                               Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                                     If there is a local dotnet tool installation 
                                                    (with a ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for 
                                                    the beam tool, then any global invocation of 
                                                    the beam tool will automatically redirect and 
                                                    call the local version. However, there will be 
                                                    a performance penalty due to the extra process 
                                                    invocation. This option flag will cause an 
                                                    error to occur instead of automatically 
                                                    redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                                    invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation                  By default, any local CLI invocation that 
                                                    should trigger a Federation of any type will 
                                                    prefer locally running Microservices. However, 
                                                    if you need the CLI to use the remotely running 
                                                    Microservices, use this option to ignore 
                                                    locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                     By default, logs will automatically mask 
                                                    tokens. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    tokens will be visible in their full text. This 
                                                    is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                     By default, logs are automatically written to a 
                                                    temp file so that they can be used in an error 
                                                    case. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    logs are not written. Also, if the 
                                                    BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment variable is 
                                                    set, no log file will be written.  [default: 
                                                    False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>               a custom location for docker. By default, the 
                                                    CLI will attempt to resolve docker through its 
                                                    usual install locations. You can also use the 
                                                    BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to 
                                                    specify. 
                                                    Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                                    identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                                Out all log messages as data payloads in 
                                                    addition to however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>             additional file paths to be included when 
                                                    building a local project manifest.
  --raw                                             Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens 
                                                    by default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                          Output syntax highlighted box text. This 
                                                    happens by default when the command is not 
                                                    piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                       a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

Raw Output:
  Returns a stream of ContentSyncResult object on the stream stream, which each may resemble the following...
  {
    "ts": 1774693741419,
    "type": "stream",
    "data": {
      "Reports": null
    }
  }
  
  Returns a single message of ContentProgressUpdateData object on the progressStream stream, which each may resemble the following...
  {
    "ts": 1774693741481,
    "type": "progressStream",
    "data": {
      "EventType": 0,
      "contentName": null,
      "errorMessage": null,
      "totalItems": 0,
      "processedItems": 0
    }
  }

  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS

  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a

                                             automatically redirect and call the local version.

                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case.

                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.

                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify.

                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

  add <tag>  Adds tag to content
  rm <tag>   Removes tag from content
  set <tag>  Set tag to content

                                                    the beam tool, then any global invocation of 
                                                    the beam tool will automatically redirect and 
                                                    call the local version. However, there will be 
                                                    a performance penalty due to the extra process 
                                                    invocation. This option flag will cause an 
                                                    error to occur instead of automatically 
                                                    redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                                    invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation                  By default, any local CLI invocation that 
                                                    should trigger a Federation of any type will 
                                                    prefer locally running Microservices. However, 
                                                    if you need the CLI to use the remotely running 
                                                    Microservices, use this option to ignore 
                                                    locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                     By default, logs will automatically mask 
                                                    tokens. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    tokens will be visible in their full text. This 
                                                    is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                     By default, logs are automatically written to a 
                                                    temp file so that they can be used in an error 
                                                    case. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    logs are not written. Also, if the 
                                                    BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment variable is 
                                                    set, no log file will be written.  [default: 
                                                    False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>               a custom location for docker. By default, the 
                                                    CLI will attempt to resolve docker through its 
                                                    usual install locations. You can also use the 
                                                    BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to 
                                                    specify. 
                                                    Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                                    identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                                Out all log messages as data payloads in 
                                                    addition to however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>             additional file paths to be included when 
                                                    building a local project manifest.
  --raw                                             Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens 
                                                    by default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                          Output syntax highlighted box text. This 
                                                    happens by default when the command is not 
                                                    piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                       a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Set tag to content

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools content tag set [<tag>] [options]

Arguments:
  <tag>  List of tags for us to affect

Options:
  --clear                                           Set the tags to a empty value, if you pass 
                                                    something in [<tag>] it will be ignored
  --filter-type                                     Defines the semantics for the `filter` 
  <ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects 
  hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
                                                    ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters
                                                    Regexes => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content whose Id is matched by any of the 
                                                    ','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
                                                    TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any 
                                                    content of the ','-separated list of filters 
                                                    (content type strings with full hierarchy --- 
                                                    StartsWith comparison)
                                                    Tags => Will add the given tags to any content 
                                                    that currently has any of the ','-separated 
                                                    list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]
  --filter <filter>                                 Accepts different strings to filter which 
                                                    content files will be affected. See the 
                                                    `filter-type` option []
  --manifest-ids <manifest-ids>                     Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids 
                                                    for which to return data. By default, will 
                                                    return just the global manifest [default: 
                                                    global]

                                                    possible without making any network calls

                                                    environment. Needed for private cloud customers

                                                    Ignorable by everyone else. Stored in 
                                                    '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>                     The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>                   A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It 
                                                    overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                                    auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                               Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                                     If there is a local dotnet tool installation 
                                                    (with a ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for 
                                                    the beam tool, then any global invocation of 
                                                    the beam tool will automatically redirect and 
                                                    call the local version. However, there will be 
                                                    a performance penalty due to the extra process 
                                                    invocation. This option flag will cause an 
                                                    error to occur instead of automatically 
                                                    redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                                    invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation                  By default, any local CLI invocation that 
                                                    should trigger a Federation of any type will 
                                                    prefer locally running Microservices. However, 
                                                    if you need the CLI to use the remotely running 
                                                    Microservices, use this option to ignore 
                                                    locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                     By default, logs will automatically mask 
                                                    tokens. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    tokens will be visible in their full text. This 
                                                    is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                     By default, logs are automatically written to a 
                                                    temp file so that they can be used in an error 
                                                    case. However, when this option is enabled, 
                                                    logs are not written. Also, if the 
                                                    BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment variable is 
                                                    set, no log file will be written.  [default: 
                                                    False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>               a custom location for docker. By default, the 
                                                    CLI will attempt to resolve docker through its 
                                                    usual install locations. You can also use the 
                                                    BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to 
                                                    specify.

                                                    identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                                Out all log messages as data payloads in

Options:
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible

  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account);

  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  get            Get a specific deployment
  list, ls       List the deployments
  plan           Plan a deployment for later release
  fire, release  Perform a release, this will modify remote running services
  ps, status     Show the current deployment status

Description:

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools deployment release [options]

Options:
  -c, --comment <comment>                          Associates this comment along with the published 
                                                   Manifest. You'll be able to read it via the 
                                                   Beamable Portal []
  -sc, --service-comments <service-comments>       Any number of strings in the format 
                                                   BeamoId::Comment
                                                   Associates each comment to the given Beamo Id if 
                                                   it's among the published services. You'll be 
                                                   able to read it via the Beamable Portal []
