  -i, --from-manifest-id, --manifest-id            A manifest id to download and use to create a 
  <from-manifest-id>                               plan
  -h, --health, --run-health-checks                Run health checks on services
  --redeploy, --restart, --roll                    Restart existing deployed services
  -bs, --build-sequentially                        Build services sequentially instead of all 
                                                   together
  --merge                                          Create a Release that merges your current local 
                                                   environment to the existing remote services. 
                                                   Existing deployed services will not be removed
  --replace                                        Create a Release that completely overrides the 
                                                   existing remote services. Existing deployed 
                                                   services that are not present locally will be 
                                                   removed (default)

                                                   .beamable folder exists, then the

      "success": false,
      "plan": null,
      "planPath": null
    }

                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally

                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this

Description:

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools deployment plan [options]

Options:
  -c, --comment <comment>                          Associates this comment along with the published 
                                                   Manifest. You'll be able to read it via the 
                                                   Beamable Portal []
  -sc, --service-comments <service-comments>       Any number of strings in the format 
                                                   BeamoId::Comment
                                                   Associates each comment to the given Beamo Id if 
                                                   it's among the published services. You'll be 
                                                   able to read it via the Beamable Portal []

  <from-manifest-id>                               plan
  -h, --health, --run-health-checks                Run health checks on services
  --redeploy, --restart, --roll                    Restart existing deployed services
  -bs, --build-sequentially                        Build services sequentially instead of all 
                                                   together
  --merge                                          Create a Release that merges your current local 
                                                   environment to the existing remote services. 
                                                   Existing deployed services will not be removed
  --replace                                        Create a Release that completely overrides the 
                                                   existing remote services. Existing deployed 
                                                   services that are not present locally will be 
                                                   removed (default)

                                                   .beamable/.. will be used. If no .sln is found, 
                                                   the .sln path will be <name>.sln. If no

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>                  A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It

Commands:

                              criteria is the oldest to the newer
  copy-state                  Copy the inventory and stats state from a user to another
  create-user                 Create a single user that can be from a template or just create new 
                              empty user
  create-user-batch           Create multiple users from multiples templates doing the request in 
                              parallel
  ps                          The ps commands manage the files for the developer users folders, it 
                              can keep watching changes in the file system and emit events to the 
                              engine

                              from the portal
  save-user                   Create a new developer user in the local files
  update-info                 Update the info of the developer user (Alias, Description etc)

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Clean up the captured users files based on a rolling buffer, the criteria is the oldest to the 
  newer

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools developer-user-manager clean-captured-user-buffer [options]

Options:
  --rolling-buffer-size <rolling-buffer-size>  The max amount of captured users that you can have 
                                               before starting to delete the oldest (0 means 
                                               infinity)
  --dryrun                                     [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                               without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                  CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                               defaults to whatever is in 
                                               '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                            If passed, sets the engine integration that is 
                                               calling for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>    The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                               Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>            The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                  PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> 
                                               Any Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                               '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                  When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                               arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                               [default: False]
  --host <host>                                This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                               Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                               exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by 
                                               everyone else. Stored in 
                                               '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>                The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                               overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                               auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>              A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It 
                                               overwrites the logged in user stored in 
                                               auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                          Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                                If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                               ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                               then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                               automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                               However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                               the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                               cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                               redirecting the execution to a new process 
                                               invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation             By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                               trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                               running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI 
                                               to use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                               option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                                By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                               However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                               visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                                By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                               file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                               However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                               written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG 
                                               environment variable is set, no log file will be 
                                               written.  [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>          a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI 
                                               will attempt to resolve docker through its usual 
                                               install locations. You can also use the 
                                               BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to specify. 
                                               Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                               identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                           Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                               however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>        additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                               local project manifest.
  --raw                                        Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                               default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                     Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                               default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                  a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                               Show help and usage information

    "data": {
      "CreatedUsers": [],
      "DeletedUsers": [],
      "UpdatedUsers": [],
      "SavedUsers": []
    }
  }

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Sets the local settings for any particular federation

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools federation local-settings set [command] [options]

Options:
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

  IFederatedGameServer  Get the local settings for the Beamable.Common.IFederatedGameServer`1[T]

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case.

                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.

                                             identified. [default: docker]

                                             local project manifest.

  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped

  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by

  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by

  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

  get    Downloads the otel collector
  ps     Starts a stream of messages containing the status of the collector

  stop   Stops the collector running process

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Starts the collector process

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools telemetry collector start [options]

Options:
  --detach                                   If it is false the collector process will run 
                                             indefinitely [default: True]
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add-paths                                     Add extra paths and ignore paths for services
  add-unity-project <path>                      Add a unity project to this beamable cli project
  add-unreal-project <path>                     Add a unreal project to this beamable cli project
  build                                         Build and monitor for compile errors
  dependencies <name>                           List project dependencies
  deps                                          Allow access to dependencies related commands
  disable                                       Disables a project. This modifies the BeamEnabled 
                                                setting in the given project files
  enable                                        Enables a project. This modifies the BeamEnabled 
                                                setting in the given project files
  generate                                      Commands that allows generating various clients for 
                                                Beamable services
  generate-client-oapi                          Generate Client Code from OAPI specifications
  generate-ignore-file <Git|None|Perforce|Svn>  Generate an ignore file in .beamable folder for 
                                                given VCS
  generate-properties <output> <beam-path>      Generates a Directory.Build.props file with the 
  <solution-dir>                                beam path and solution dir
  group                                         List Service Groups
  list                                          Get a list of microservices
  logs <service>                                Tail the logs of a microservice
  new                                           Commands that allows creating new Beamable services
  oapi                                          Generate the Open API specification for the project
  open                                          Open the solution file for all services
  open-mongo <service-name>                     Opens a Mongo-Express web page for the given mongo 
                                                storage object []
  open-swagger, swagger <service-name>          Opens the swagger page for a given service []
  ps                                            List the running status of local services not 
                                                running in docker
  regenerate <name> <output> <copy-path>        Regenerate the solution csproj, Dockerfile and 
                                                Program.cs files [, ]
  remove                                        Removes the local source code for a service or 
                                                storage
  run                                           Run a project
  share-code <source>                           Given a dll, copy the dll to the associated unity 
                                                projects
  stop                                          Stop a running service
  storage                                       Commands for managing storage data
  update-unity-beam-package <path>              Updates or adds Beamable packages to Unity project

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Allow access to dependencies related commands

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools project deps [command] [options]

Options:
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <microservice> <dependency>     Add a given storage as a dependency of a microservice
  list                                Lists all dependencies of given service, if none then lists 
                                      all dependencies of all existing services
  remove <microservice> <dependency>  Remove the dependency between the given microservice and 
                                      storage

                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically

                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.

                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Create common library project that later can be connected to the services

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools project new common-lib <name> [options]

Arguments:
  <name>  The name of the new library project

Options:
  --sln <sln>                                Relative path to the .sln file to use for the new 
                                             project. If the .sln file does not exist, it will be 
                                             created. When no option is configured, if this command 
                                             is executing inside a .beamable folder, then the first 
                                             .sln found in .beamable/.. will be used. If no .sln is 
                                             found, the .sln path will be <name>.sln. If no 
                                             .beamable folder exists, then the 
                                             <project>/<project>.sln will be used []
  --output-path <output-path>                The path where the project is going to be created
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json

  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp

CLI Version: 7.0.0


Description:
  Commands for managing the CLI version

Usage:
  Beamable.Tools version [command] [options]

Options:
  --dryrun                                   [DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible 
                                             without making any network calls
  --cid <cid>                                CID (CustomerId) to use (found in Portal->Account); 
                                             defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --engine <engine>                          If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling 
                                             for the command
  --engine-sdk-version <engine-sdk-version>  The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that 
                                             Engine
  --engine-version <engine-version>          The version of the engine that is calling the CLI
  --pid <pid>                                PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any 
                                             Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in 
                                             '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  -q, --quiet                                When true, skip input waiting and use default 
                                             arguments (or error if no defaults are possible) 
                                             [default: False]
  --host <host>                              This option defines the target Beamable environment. 
                                             Needed for private cloud customers to target their 
                                             exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone 
                                             else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'
  --access-token <access-token>              The access token to use for the requests. It 
                                             overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json 
                                             for THIS INVOCATION ONLY
  --refresh-token <refresh-token>            A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites 
                                             the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS 
                                             INVOCATION ONLY
  --log, --logs <log>                        Extra logs gets printed out
  --no-redirect                              If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a 
                                             ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool, 
                                             then any global invocation of the beam tool will 
                                             automatically redirect and call the local version. 
                                             However, there will be a performance penalty due to 
                                             the extra process invocation. This option flag will 
                                             cause an error to occur instead of automatically 
                                             redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.
  -prf, --prefer-remote-federation           By default, any local CLI invocation that should 
                                             trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally 
                                             running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to 
                                             use the remotely running Microservices, use this 
                                             option to ignore locally running services.
  --unmask-logs                              By default, logs will automatically mask tokens. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be 
                                             visible in their full text. This is a security risk.
  --no-log-file                              By default, logs are automatically written to a temp 
                                             file so that they can be used in an error case. 
                                             However, when this option is enabled, logs are not 
                                             written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment 
                                             variable is set, no log file will be written.  
                                             [default: False]
  --docker-cli-path <docker-cli-path>        a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will 
                                             attempt to resolve docker through its usual install 
                                             locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE 
                                             environment variable to specify. 
                                             Currently, a docker path has been automatically 
                                             identified. [default: docker]
  --emit-log-streams                         Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to 
                                             however they are logged
  --add-project-path <add-project-path>      additional file paths to be included when building a 
                                             local project manifest.
  --raw                                      Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is being piped
  --pretty                                   Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by 
                                             default when the command is not piped
  --dotnet-path <dotnet-path>                a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]
